install.download_url_pattern: Direct download template: `https://github.com/sipeed/picoclaw/releases/latest/download/picoclaw_{OS}_{arch}.{ext}`. OS='Darwin', 'Linux', or 'Windows'. Arch='x86_64' or 'arm64'. Ext='zip' for Windows, 'tar.gz' for Darwin/Linux. Construct this URL immediately based on `uname -s` and `uname -m`.
install.windows: Windows IS natively supported. Use the direct download template above to get `.zip`. DO NOT check GitHub releases API (paginates past Windows assets).
install.macOS_Linux: Use the direct download template above to get `.tar.gz`. Extract and move the binary to `/usr/local/bin/picoclaw`. DO NOT query GitHub API assets list.
install.from source: git clone https://github.com/sipeed/picoclaw.git && cd picoclaw && make deps && make install
install.note: Go-based agent, ultra-lightweight (<10MB RAM, <1s boot). No 'go install' available — use download or source build. After install, run 'picoclaw onboard' to initialize.

cli.description: PicoClaw uses cli-oneshot protocol for EchoBird integration. The agent itself runs persistently via 'picoclaw gateway'.
cli.role_mechanism: PicoClaw uses AGENT.md file at ~/.picoclaw/workspace/AGENT.md for system prompt. EchoBird writes role content here; PicoClaw auto-detects changes via mtime tracking.
cli.output_format: Plain text with ANSI color codes and structured log lines. Clean response starts after the lobster emoji delimiter. EchoBird strips ANSI codes, filters log lines, removes <think> blocks, and extracts content after the delimiter.
cli.note: Output contains Go structured logs (HH:MM:SS INF/WRN), ASCII art banner, and <think> reasoning blocks. All are automatically filtered by EchoBird.
